title: Man arrested for alleged assault of rideshare passenger at Logan Airport
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Leonard Bacon, 23, of Lowell, was arrested for allegedly assaulting a rideshare passenger at Logan Airport in Boston.
The incident occurred around 5:30 a.m. Friday; the victim was stabbed and sustained serious but non-life-threatening injuries.
Bacon faces charges of assault and battery with a dangerous weapon and is due in East Boston District Court.

title: WBZ meteorologist returns after taking 3 years away to care for her son with cancer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: WBZ meteorologist Sarah Wroblewski returns to work after nearly three years caring for her son Declan with brain cancer.
Declan Wroblewski, diagnosed with stage 4 medulloblastoma in 2023, is no longer in active cancer treatment as announced by Sarah.
Sarah Wroblewski will primarily deliver weekend weather forecasts while Declan adjusts to a more stable routine with ongoing support.

title: 2 killed in crash on Route 6 near Westport-Dartmouth town line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two people died in a Route 6 crash near the Westport-Dartmouth line involving a Toyota Camry and Saab sedan.
The crash victims, Tristan Bedient and Kate Aldrich, suffered fatal injuries and were pronounced dead at a local hospital.
The investigation is active, involving Massachusetts State Police and local law enforcement, with no life-threatening injuries to Saab occupants.

title: Bus driver strike ends in Merrimack Valley after nearly 2 weeks
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Merrimack Valley Transit drivers and mechanics ended a 10-day strike after agreeing to a one-year contract.
The agreement includes a $4 hourly pay raise and maintains Saturday overtime for the transit workers.
Bus service across Massachusetts towns, including Lawrence and Andover, resumed, providing fare-free rides after the strike.

title: 5 takeaways as red-hot Red Sox rally past Mets, 3-2, for 9th straight victory
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox defeat the New York Mets 3-2 in extra innings, marking their ninth consecutive victory.
Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Garrett Whitlock maintain strong late-game performances with ERAs of 2.20 and 2.18, respectively.
Boston sweeps a nine-game road trip for the second time in franchise history, improving to 14-2 in their last 16 games.

title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan inducted into Fenway Music Hall of Fame after completing a historic four-night run at Fenway Park.
Kahan joins Billy Joel, Paul McCartney, and Zac Brown Band as the fourth inductee, announced by his mother during his final show.
His celebration included fireworks and appearances by Boston sports figures, marking a successful year with album release and media features.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from a $45 million settlement with Cash App over alleged deceptive practices.
The settlement addresses claims that Cash App misled users about app safety and failed to protect them from fraud.
Block Inc., Cash App's parent company, must enhance user protections and customer support under the settlement terms.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old Tyngsborough man allegedly took a police boat and activated its emergency lights on Lake Mascuppic.
He is expected to face a summons in Lowell District Court for impersonating a police officer, authorities said.
Following the incident, officials removed the police boat from the dock to prevent further unauthorized access.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: More than 60 international tall ships and military vessels arrived in Boston Harbor for the six-day Sail Boston 250 event.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail, with ships representing over 20 countries docking across Boston.
Thousands of visitors attended the event, celebrating Boston’s maritime history and witnessing the final stop of the Sail250 tour.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox selected shortstop Jake Schaffner from UNC with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner hit .356 with six home runs, 50 RBI, and stole 26 bases during UNC's College World Series run.
Defensively, Schaffner committed only three errors in 68 games and may move to second base due to arm strength.
